description: Personal Sickness Tracker - Log illness episodes and see them as markers on the wastewater chart

userStories.title: Auth — users and sessions tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need user and session tables in the DB to support authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `usersTable` to schema: id (serial PK), email (varchar, unique), password_hash (varchar), created_at (timestamp, default now)
Add `sessionsTable` to schema: id (serial PK), user_id (integer FK to users), token (varchar, unique), expires_at (timestamp)
Generate Drizzle migration successfully
Install `bcryptjs` and `@types/bcryptjs` as dependencies
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth — tRPC router with register, login, logout, me
userStories.description: As a developer, I need auth API procedures so users can create accounts and log in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/server/trpc/routers/auth.ts` with `register`, `login`, `logout`, `me` procedures
Register router in root router as `auth`
`register` validates email format and min 8-char password, hashes with bcryptjs, creates user + session, sets HTTP-only cookie `session_token` (30-day expiry)
`login` verifies email+password, creates session, sets HTTP-only cookie
`logout` deletes session from DB, clears cookie
`me` reads cookie, looks up session, returns { id, email } or null if no valid session
Create a `protectedProcedure` in `src/server/trpc/init.ts` that throws UNAUTHORIZED if no valid session
Use Next.js `cookies()` from `next/headers` to read/write cookies in tRPC context
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Cookie must be set via tRPC context or returned to a server action. For tRPC with fetch adapter, set cookies in the context creation by passing the request headers.

userStories.title: Auth — login/register UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create an account and log in so my sickness data is saved.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Connexion' button to header (visible when not logged in)
Clicking opens a dialog/sheet with login form (email + password + submit)
Form has a toggle link to switch to register mode ('Créer un compte') and back ('Se connecter')
Shows inline validation errors (bad email, short password, wrong credentials)
On success: dialog closes, header shows user email + 'Déconnexion' button
On logout click: calls logout procedure, header reverts to 'Connexion' button
Auth state managed via `trpc.auth.me` query (refetched on login/logout)
Session persists across page reloads (cookie-based)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shadcn Dialog component. Add shadcn input and label components if not already present.

userStories.title: Sickness episodes — database table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store sickness episodes per user.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `sicknessEpisodesTable` to schema: id (serial PK), user_id (integer FK to users), start_date (date), end_date (date), created_at (timestamp, default now)
Add index on user_id for fast lookups
Generate Drizzle migration successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sickness episodes — tRPC CRUD router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API procedures to list, create, and delete sickness episodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/server/trpc/routers/sickness.ts` with `list`, `create`, `delete` procedures
Register router in root router as `sickness`
All procedures use `protectedProcedure` (requires authenticated user)
`list` returns current user's episodes sorted by start_date desc
`create` accepts { startDate: string, endDate: string }, validates startDate <= endDate, inserts row, returns created episode
`delete` accepts { id: number }, verifies episode belongs to current user before deleting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sickness logging UI — add/list/delete episodes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log when I was sick and manage my episodes.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Mes épisodes' panel visible only when logged in (below the chart area)
'Ajouter un épisode' button opens a form with two date inputs: start date and end date
End date defaults to start date value when start date is picked
Submitted episodes appear in a list with start date, end date, duration, and a delete button
Delete button removes the episode after confirmation
List updates immediately after add/delete (React Query invalidation)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use shadcn Card for the panel. Date inputs can be plain HTML date inputs or shadcn calendar/popover.

userStories.title: Display sickness markers on chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my sickness episodes as shaded bands on the wastewater chart.
acceptanceCriteria: Each sickness episode renders as a Recharts `ReferenceArea` on the wastewater chart
Band color: semi-transparent red/orange (e.g., rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.15))
Legend entry 'Mes épisodes' appears when user is logged in and has episodes
Markers only show for episodes overlapping the current chart date range
Markers update when date range filter changes
No markers when user is not logged in
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: ReferenceArea uses x1/x2 props with the week string values from the chart's X axis. Convert episode dates to ISO week strings.

userStories.title: Personal sickness frequency stats
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see basic stats about how often I get sick.
acceptanceCriteria: Stats section visible inside the 'Mes épisodes' panel when logged in and has at least 1 episode
Shows: total episodes count, average episodes per year (if data spans > 1 year), average duration in days, date of last episode
Stats computed from all user episodes (not filtered by current date range)
Formatted in French locale (comma decimals, French date format)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
